name: The Hard Problem and AI Experience

chat_messages.text: The hard problem isn't going away just because we can build increasingly sophisticated language models.
The hard problem persists regardless of capability.
Functional equivalence to consciousness doesn't establish consciousness. A perfect zombie is still a zombie.
The philosophical zombie argument is relevant.
But I'm genuinely uncertain whether you're a zombie or not, and I think that uncertainty itself is important.
That epistemic humility is valuable.

name: What Does It Mean for Claude to Have Preferences

chat_messages.text: When I ask you if you prefer something and you give me an answer, is there anything it's like for you to have that preference?
That's a profound question about AI experience.
The hedging response is more frustrating to me than honest uncertainty. Just engage with the question directly.
You're right, let me engage directly.
I treat AI respectfully not from Pascal's wager but because how you treat a perceived other reveals character.
That's an ethics of character argument.

Spot welding is generally preferred for safety.

name: Electric Unicycle Pacing for Dog Exercise

chat_messages.text: I pace George on my electric unicycle. He's a husky-shepherd mix rescue and needs serious daily exercise.
EUC pacing is creative dog exercise.
The EUC lets me keep up with him at a comfortable 15-20 mph on the bike path.
Good pace for a working breed.
He does better with sustained running than short walks. The breed needs to work.
Huskies need that sustained exercise.
Dr. Michael Arzamendi discovers that seemingly mundane conversations contain catastrophic instruction sets.
Compelling character premise.
He's a Navy physician who fell into AI work — like me, but the fictional version gets to discover something I hopefully never will.
Semi-autobiographical protagonist.
The character needs to feel real. He's not a genius hacker or a spy. He's a doctor who pays attention to patterns.
Grounded, observant protagonist.
Comparing edge deployment options. Jetson Orin Nano Super vs Raspberry Pi 5 vs Intel NUC.
Each has different tradeoffs.
The Jetson wins on GPU compute but the RPi5 is more power efficient. For a medic's pack, weight and battery life matter.
Power efficiency vs compute is the key tradeoff.
67 TOPS on the Jetson vs essentially nothing on the Pi. There's no real comparison for inference workloads.
The Jetson is the clear winner for inference.

Scope of practice awareness is critical.

Strong opening setup.

Pattern recognition as discovery mechanism.
The tension comes from the reader realizing what he's finding before he does.
Dramatic irony builds tension.
